arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-addr2line llvm-symbolizer 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'binutils|llvm' | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try downloading the debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
# BuildId: 4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801
BUILDID="4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801"

# Try SSQP/SymbolCheckKey format for ELF debug info
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so"
echo ""
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
echo ""
# Also try the NuGet symbol package format
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so.dbg/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so.dbg"
echo ""
arguments.description: Check symbol server for debug symbols

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Check symbol server for debug symbols

data.reasoningText:
The tombstone shows a crash in a .NET Android app (`com.companyname.CoreClrApp`) with a SIGSEGV (null pointer dereference at offset 0x48). The backtrace has several frames in `libcoreclr.so` with BuildId `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`.

To symbolicate these frames, I need to find the debug symbols for `libcoreclr.so` with that specific BuildId. The BuildId is `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`.


This is the CoreCLR runtime library, and Microsoft publishes symbols for .NET runtime on their symbol server. I can query the symbol server at `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols` using the BuildId to locate the debug symbols for this specific version of the library. I need to try a different approach here. Instead of guessing at the symbol server URL format, let me check what debugging tools are actually available and use those to symbolicate the addresses directly.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-addr2line llvm-symbolizer 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'binutils|llvm' | head -5

# Try downloading the debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

BUILDID="4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801"

# Try SSQP/SymbolCheckKey format for ELF debug info
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so"
echo ""
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
echo ""
# Also try the NuGet symbol package format
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so.dbg/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so.dbg"
echo ""
arguments.description: Check symbol server for debug symbols
